methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nuclearn.ai is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $10.5M to date. The nuclear energy sector includes three public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private peers, though the private set spans multiple stages and valuations, limiting direct peer benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total raised ($10.5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields $21M. This reflects the company's advancement beyond seed but acknowledges the execution risk inherent at Series A, where revenue traction and technology validation remain nascent.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable here. While eight nuclear energy peers exist, the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar valuation tier yielded insufficient overlap (fewer than three datapoints). Relying on all-sector medians would obscure sector-specific funding dynamics and introduce material noise.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one methodologically sound input, supports a median valuation of $21M with a range of $18M–$24M. The range reflects typical Series A volatility absent richer comparable data. Key uncertainties include technology readiness level, regulatory pathway clarity, and the ability to differentiate within a crowded nuclear subsector. A narrower, more defensible range would require additional operational metrics or closer-stage peer discovery.
